cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the north in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and to the northeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is unknown, but may be related to faint field line movement visible over the northeast limb in SDO AIA 171 starting around 2026-06-06T03:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is a far-sided eruption visible on the southwest limb in STEREO A EUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-06-06T13:15Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible as a southeast partial halo in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and is seen to the east in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption and M1.8 flare from Active Region 14461 (S22E24) starting around 2025-06-06T13:45Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery. An opening of field lines is visible over the southeast limb in STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery, and a large EUV wave and dimming is observed surrounding the eruption site in SDO AIA 193 and GOES SUVI 195/284 imagery. Additionally, post eruptive arcades are visible in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/105 imagery starting around 2026-06-06T15:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated bulk measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Shock measurement following the outermost faint feature observed in GOES CCOR-1 imagery. A default half-width of 45 degrees was used as the shock feature is not observed in STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Preliminary bulk measurement based on the source longitude.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not seen in STEREO A COR2. The source may be field line movement in SDO AIA starting around 2026-06-06T16:34Z over the E limb around S10, or AR 14464 with slow brightening near S09E63, the movement is not too clear where it traces on-disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 3D analysis using E63 as the likely observed source longitude.

cmes.note: CME observed to the S in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 during a data gap spanning 2026-06-06T19:09Z to 2026-06-06T23:09Z. The source is a filament eruption far-sided from Earth, observed in STEREO A EUVI 304 at 2026-06-06T19:15Z as filament ejecta on/near its west limb, putting it at approximately W150 with STEREO A currently located at W60. Also observed as filament ejecta over the S limb in GOES SUVI 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative analysis of the shock front associated with this CME, using only GOES CCOR-1 due to its good view of the shock front and lack of STEREO A COR2 to triangulate with. W150 is used as the approximate source of the observed filament, and the width could greatly vary as well due to the partial halo single spacecraft analysis.

SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only fit due to the lack of STEREO A COR2 visibility to triangulate. W150 is used as the approximate source of the observed filament, and the lat/lon could vary. Higher confidence in the southward deflection out of the ecliptic plane, regardless.

cmes.note: Bright, relatively narrow CME seen to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2A, along with GOES CCOR-1 likely associated with a far-sided eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using all available spacecraft for triangulation of the longitude.

cmes.note: Narrow CME seen to the southeast seen in STEREO Ahead with seemingly no corresponding match in GOES and SOHO coronagraphs, likely associated with a far-sided eruption relative to the Earth-Sun line.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO Ahead plane of sky with just a few frames.

cmes.note: Minor CME seen to the northeast in a few frames of STEREO Ahead coronagraphs before it quickly disappears with unknown originating source.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO Ahead plane of sky measurement, as the source is unknown and the CME is not well observed in SOHO or GOES coronagraphs.

cmes.note: CME characterized by a wide, dark front seen to the west in STEREO Ahead, SOHO, and GOES coronagraphs with unknown source, perhaps in association with eruptive activity taking place over the STEREO Ahead western limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge using three spacecraft (SOHO, STEREO Ahead, GOES) for triangulation of the longitude. High uncertainty in longitude with error in longitude up to +/- 30 degrees.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the South by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-07T19:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a reduced campaign cadence. The source of this event is faint dimming seen centered at approx. S40E20 beginning at approx. 2026-06-07T18:25Z as seen in SDO AIA 193 and 211.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint CME seen to the South by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-07T22:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-06-07T20:08Z. The source of this event is minor dimming and field line movement seen centered at approx. S40W20 beginning at approx. 2026-06-07T21:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 171, 193 and 211.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASC C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in STEREO A COR2, and to the SSE in SOHO LASCO C2 and faintly in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely on or near STEREO A's SW limb, currently around W150 with STEREO A at W60.2, observed in STEREO A EUVI 304 as filament ejecta starting around 2026-06-08T01:45Z. Although a bright spot on-disk is briefly observed with this filament ejecta on the limb, around W150, triangulation puts the front around E178, so the source is either further around the limb or deflected.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, even more faintly in SOHO LASCO C3/GOES CCOR-1 due to it fading into the background after early frames. The source is likely an area of field line movement and dimming centered around S08E42, near AR 14464 (S12E35) starting around 2026-06-08T04:25Z in SDO AIA 171/193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, noting the poor image quality in STEREO A COR2 due on an ongoing campaign along with the faint leading edge providing some uncertainty.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in STEREO A COR2 and SSW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is very likely far-sided from Earth due to triangulation, likely visible in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 at 2026-06-08T07:25Z as field line dimming and movement on or near its SW limb, which is located around W150 with STEREO A at W60.3.

cmeAnalyses.note: Bulk triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and two available frames of STEREO A COR2. Front appears slightly more wide in COR2A than C2/C3.

Shock front triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. With the poor image quality in STEREO A COR2 due to a campaign, W162 is used as the bulk longitude source and varying the parameters from there. Tracking the width via CCOR-1's view of the SE/S/SW expanding faint shock front, but the width could vary due to the uncertainty.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-09T07:12Z, but fades before exiting the field-of-view and is therefore not seen in other coronagraphs. The source of this event is field line opening seen from beyond the NNW limb in SDO AIA 171 and GOES SUVI 284 beginning at approx. 2026-06-09T06:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using a longitude of W90 due to source along the limb and lack of imagery for triangulation.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the West by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-06-09T09:08Z. This CME is not seen by SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1 likely because it is completely far sided from their POV. The source of this event is likely an eruption from beyond the STEREO A West limb, as field line opening and post eruptive arcades can be seen in this vicinity in STEREO A EUVI 195 beginning at approx. 2026-06-09T09:00Z. This CME is only visible for a few STEREO A COR2 frames due to its reduced campaign cadence.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A plane-of-sky using a longitude of W150.4 (STEREO is currently located at W60.4) due to source along STEREO A's West limb and lack of imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: The source of this event is a filament eruption seen originating from the vicinity of Active Region 14463 (N17E15) beginning at approximately 2026-06-09T15:46Z. Filament material can be seen in SDO AIA, STEREO A EUVI and GOES SUVI 304. A wide region of dimming, field line opening and a slight EUV wave is also visible in SDO AIA 171, 193, 211, STEREO A EUVI 195 and GOES SUVI 284 appearing to exhibit heavy Eastern deflection, possibly due to the eruptions proximity to a coronal hole.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Wide CME first seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-09T21:12Z. It is also seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is most likely wide moving field lines seen from beyond the W limb in GOES SUVI 284 beginning around 2026-06-09T20:36Z. Movement beyond the W limb is also seen in STEREO A EUVI 195 beginning as early as 2026-06-09T19:35Z. In coronagraphs, this CME partially overlaps with CME: 2026-06-09T21:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was chosen because the source is beyond the W limb but without visual confirmation of location of eruption.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-09T21:36Z. It is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The is most likely an eruption from AR 14461 (S21W19) that appears to be heavily deflected southward likely due its proximity to a coronal hole that was centered around N00W05. The eruption is best seen in SDO AIA 193 as moving field lines and a large area of dimming directed toward the south. The eruption is also accompanied by C2.3 class flare from AR 14461 that peaked at 2026-06-09T19:18Z best seen in SDO AIA 131. In coronagraphs, this CME partially overlaps with far-sided CME: 2026-06-09T21:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative measurement based on the visible source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the Northeast in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-10T18:00Z, but the leading edge quickly fades before exiting the field-of-view in C2 and is therefore not clearly seen in other coronagraphs. The source of this event is field line opening seen from beyond the NE limb in GOES SUVI 284 beginning at approx. 2026-06-10T17:24Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using a longitude of E90 due to source near the limb and lack of coronagraph imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW in STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-06-10T18:08Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. The source of this CME is a filament eruption seen from beyond the West limb (approx. N15W150) in STEREO A EUVI 304 and 195 beginning at approx. 2026-06-10T17:25Z. The Solar Orbiter STIX instrument observed an estimated M7.0 [M3.9 - X1.3] peaking at 2026-06-10T17:17Z which may be related to this event. This CME overlaps with CME: 2026-06-10T18:12Z in STEREO A COR2.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the South by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-10T18:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this CME is field line opening seen off the SW limb (approx. S25W150) in STEREO A EUVI 195 beginning at approx. 2026-06-10T17:35Z, indicating a far sided event. This CME overlaps with CME: 2026-06-10T18:08Z, which is also the result of an eruption from beyond the West limb in STEREO A, but is further North.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Bright loop CME and shock first seen to the Northeast by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-11T00:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this CME is a long duration C6.7 class flare from Active Region 14465 (N09E39) seen peaking at 2026-06-11T00:02Z. This flare can be seen in SDO AIA 131 along with a wide region of dimming, field line opening and an EUV wave seen in SDO AIA 171, 193, 211 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: UPDATED BULK parameters using later frames in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Updated shock measurement based on the best fit between the outermost wide feature seen in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery and a majority of the widest feature seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery, excluding small portions of the upper and lower sections of the shock feature.
PRELIMINARY BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
PRELIMINARY SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the east/southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption and opening of field lines visible over the eastern limb in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195 imagery starting around 2026-06-11T07:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint, thin CME observed to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. The source is likely associated with a C9.0 flare (N10E34) from AR 14465 peaking at 2026-06-11T08:28Z, and a larger area of dimming centered nearby (N12E28) observed in SDO AIA 193 triggered with the flare.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between STEREO A COR2 which views the front better, and SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the E only in STEREO A COR2. The source is likely dimming in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-06-11T08:48Z centered around S25W32.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A COR2 only 3D fit using the observed source centered at W32. The front is very faint and difficult to resolve even in running difference imagery, and parameters may vary as a result of this uncertainty, but it does evolve slowly regardless.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the E only in STEREO A COR2, likely due to being occulted by the GOES CCOR-1 and/or SOHO LASCO C2/C3 occulting disks, or missed by a SOHO LASCO C2/C3 downlink delay starting at 2026-06-11T19:36Z. The source may be AR 14465, as a weak filament eruption and field line movement centered around N08EE starting around 2026-06-11T17:04Z in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A COR2 only 3D analysis using E33 as the likely source longitude.

cmes.note: CME observed to the S in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2026-06-11T19:53Z and not in SOHO LASCO C2 due to a downlink gap between approximately 2026-06-11T19:36Z to 2026-06-11T22:12Z. The source is a far-sided high southern latitude filament eruption observed in GOES SUVI 304 over the S limb at 2026-06-11T19:32Z and on/beyond the SW limb in STEREO A EUVI 304 at 2026-06-06T20:15Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: GOES CCOR-1 plane-of-sky with no STEREO A COR2 to triangulate the far-sided source.

cmes.note: Very narrow, minor CME seen to the southeast in SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2A, and GOES CCOR-1 without a very clear source; may be associated with a narrow eruption seen best in GOES SUVI 284 near 2026-06-12T00:30Z near the eastern limb, however, confidence is fairly low as the CME geometry would suggest a much further southerly latitude than this source.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary leading edge measurement using all available spacecraft and triangulation to fit the source.

cmes.note: Narrow CME seen to the southeast in STEREO A COR2A and very dimly to the due south in SOHO LASCO C2 which is likely associated with an eruption in AR 14464 near S12W20 around 2026-06-12T13:50Z seen best in SDO AIA composite imagery using the sum of 211/193/171 Angstrom imagers.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2A for triangulation, noting the eastward deflection along the Earth-Sun line due to the presence of an equatorial coronal hole near W45.

cmes.note: Faint partial halo CME seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 with preference to the east and seen more narrow with a brighter bulk and disconnected bulk/shock in STEREO Ahead COR2A with brighter bulk to the east-northeast and shock extending from the southeast to the north in association with a filament eruption near N00E35 erupting near 2026-06-12T16:15Z, best seen in SDO AIA composite imagery using the sum of 211/193/171 Angstroms and as well in GOES SUVI/SDO AIA 304 Angstroms. This CME, seen due east and northeast in COR2A, is seen overlapping the previous CME: 2026-06-12T15:38Z which is seen to the southeast and which is a separate eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement out to 10 solar radii with medium confidence using SOHO/STEREO A/GOES for triangulation; the CME appears to fit closer to the Earth-Sun line than the source at E35 given the halo-like presentation.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the SSE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-12T18:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a filament eruption likely originating from the far side seen in GOES SUVI 304 beginning at approx. 2026-06-12T18:16Z. Faint field line movement can also be seen along the SE limb in SDO AIA 171 in association with this eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Loop CME first seen to the East by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-12T19:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a filament eruption seen along the Eastern limb (approx. S10E80 with some Northern deflection) in SDO AIA /GOES SUVI 304 beginning at approx. 2026-06-12T18:56Z. Wide opening field lines and post eruptive arcades are also visible in SDO AIA 131, 171, 193, 211 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the East beginning at 2026-06-12T23:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap beginning at 2026-06-12T20:23Z. The source of this event is likely continued outflow following a large filament eruption resulting in CME: 2026-06-12T19:24Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using a longitude of E90 due to lack of clear source and no STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate.
